data.content:
### Secure Next.js Page Authorization Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/agents/knowledge-base.md Illustrates how to implement authorization checks directly within Next.js `page.tsx` files in the App Directory. This ensures that sensitive content and routes are only accessible to authorized users by validating session information before rendering protected content. It avoids placing these checks in layout components. ```tsx // app/admin/page.tsx import { redirect } from "next/navigation"; import { getUserSession } from "@/lib/auth"; export default async function AdminPage() { const session = await getUserSession(); if (!session || session.user.role !== "admin") { redirect("/"); // Or show an error } // Protected content here return
Welcome, Admin!
; } ``` -------------------------------- ### Using Managed User Access Token Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/introduction.mdx Managed user access tokens are required for managing schedules, event types, and specific booking-related endpoints. The token should be passed in the Authorization header as a Bearer token. ```APIDOC ## Using Managed User Access Token ### Description Managed user access tokens are used to authenticate requests for managing schedules, event types, and certain booking operations. The token must be included in the `Authorization` header. ### Method Any HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.) for endpoints that require managed user authentication. ### Endpoint Any Cal.com API endpoint that requires authentication for a managed user. ### Parameters #### Request Headers - **Authorization** (string) - Required - The access token for the managed user, formatted as `Bearer `. ### Request Example ``` Authorization: Bearer your_managed_user_access_token ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) Responses will vary depending on the specific endpoint being called. #### Response Example ```json { "example": "response data for the called endpoint" } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Cal.com API v1 to v2 Authentication Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/v1-v2-differences.mdx Demonstrates the shift in authentication methods from API v1's query parameter to API v2's Authorization header. API v2 also introduces the 'cal-api-version' header for version control. ```bash curl https://api.cal.com/v1/bookings?apiKey=cal_test_xxxxxx ``` ```bash curl https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings \ -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_test_xxxxxx" \ -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13" ``` -------------------------------- ### V2 API Authentication Example Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/migration-guide.mdx Shows the recommended way to authenticate API requests in Cal.com V2 using a Bearer token in the Authorization header for enhanced security. ```bash curl https://api.cal.com/v2/event-types \ -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_xxxxxx" ``` -------------------------------- ### API Authentication Header Example Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v2/introduction.mdx Demonstrates how to authenticate API requests using an Authorization header with a Bearer token. This is a common pattern for many REST APIs. ```http 'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY' ``` -------------------------------- ### Cal.com Google Calendar Callback URIs Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/apps/install-apps/google.mdx These are the authorized redirect URIs required for the Google OAuth flow within Cal.com. They specify where Google should send the user back after authentication. Ensure `` is replaced with your Cal.com instance's domain. ```text /api/integrations/googlecalendar/callback /api/auth/callback/google ``` -------------------------------- ### Refresh Managed Organization API Key (POST) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/guides/managed-orgs.mdx Use this endpoint to refresh the API key for a managed organization. You must include your current API key in the Authorization header. The request body allows you to specify the validity period for the new API key or set it to never expire. The response contains the newly generated API key. ```bash curl -X POST https://api.cal.com/v2/api-keys/refresh \ -H "Authorization: Bearer " \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"apiKeyDaysValid": 30}' ``` ```json { "status": "success", "data": { "apiKey": "cal_xxx467709fffebe8c6559211cb5f178f" } } ``` -------------------------------- ### Check Multiple Event Permissions (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/pbac/README.md A function demonstrating how to efficiently check multiple permissions related to the 'EventType' resource using `getResourcePermissions` and mapping the results. This is useful for features requiring comprehensive access checks for a single resource. ```typescript // Check if user can perform multiple operations async function canUserManageEvent(userId: number, teamId: number) { const permissionService = new PermissionCheckService(); // Get all event type permissions at once const permissions = await permissionService.getResourcePermissions({ userId, teamId, resource: Resource.EventType, }); const permissionMap = PermissionMapper.toActionMap(permissions, Resource.EventType); return { canCreate: permissionMap[CrudAction.Create] ?? false, canRead: permissionMap[CrudAction.Read] ?? false, canUpdate: permissionMap[CrudAction.Update] ?? false, canDelete: permissionMap[CrudAction.Delete] ?? false, }; } ``` -------------------------------- ### Check User Permissions (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/pbac/README.md Demonstrates how to check if a user has the 'booking.export' permission on both the server-side using `permissionService.checkPermission` and client-side via TRPC's `useQuery`. It includes fallback roles for backward compatibility. ```typescript // Server-side const canExport = await permissionService.checkPermission({ userId, teamId, permission: "booking.export", fallbackRoles: [MembershipRole.OWNER, MembershipRole.ADMIN], }); // Client-side const { data: canExport } = trpc.viewer.pbac.checkPermission.useQuery({ teamId, permission: "booking.export", }); ``` -------------------------------- ### Add Booking Export Permissions to Roles (SQL) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/pbac/README.md This SQL script adds the 'booking.export' permission to the 'admin' and optionally 'member' roles. It uses `gen_random_uuid()` for generating IDs and `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` to ensure idempotency. The 'owner' role automatically receives this permission via a wildcard. ```sql -- Add the new "booking.export" permission to admin and owner roles -- Owner role automatically gets it via wildcard (*.*) - no action needed! -- Add to admin role INSERT INTO "RolePermission" (id, "roleId", resource, action, "createdAt") VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), 'admin_role', 'booking', 'export', NOW()) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; -- Optionally add to member role if needed INSERT INTO "RolePermission" (id, "roleId", resource, action, "createdAt") VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), 'member_role', 'booking', 'export', NOW()) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING; ``` -------------------------------- ### Migrate Entity Permissions to PBAC (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/PERMISSIONS.md Demonstrates how to replace direct entity permission checks with Permission-Based Access Control (PBAC) using permission strings. This approach enhances flexibility and allows for custom role creation. It assumes the existence of a `permissionMatches` function and a `userPermissions` object. ```typescript import { TRPCError } from "@trpc/server"; // Instead of: // if (!(await canEditEntity(form, user.id))) throw new TRPCError({ code: "UNAUTHORIZED" }); // Use: // if (!permissionMatches("routingForm.update", userPermissions)) throw new TRPCError({ code: "UNAUTHORIZED" }); ``` -------------------------------- ### API Authentication Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/api-reference/v1/authentication.mdx All API requests must be made over HTTPS and authenticated using an API key provided as a query parameter. ```APIDOC ## API Authentication ### Description Authentication to the API is performed via URL Query Params. Provide your API key as a query param. ### Method GET ### Endpoint `https://api.cal.com/v1/ ### Parameters #### Query Parameters - **apiKey** (string) - Required - Your Cal.com API key. Test mode keys start with `cal_` and live mode keys start with `cal_live_`. ### Request Example ``` https://api.cal.com/v1/?apiKey=cal_live_xxxxxx ``` ### Response #### Success Response (200) - **message** (string) - A success message indicating authentication. #### Response Example ```json { "message": "Authentication successful" } ``` #### Error Response (401) - **message** (string) - An error message indicating authentication failure. #### Error Response Example ```json { "message": "Invalid API key" } ``` ``` -------------------------------- ### Get Booking Recordings (API) Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt Retrieves Cal Video recordings associated with a specific booking. This API endpoint requires the booking UID and an authorization token. The response includes details about each recording, such as its ID, room name, start time, status, and a download link. ```bash # Get all recordings for a booking curl -X GET https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings/bk_abc123xyz/recordings \ -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_***" ``` -------------------------------- ### Define Billing Manager Role Permissions (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/pbac/README.md An example array defining permissions for a 'Billing Manager' role. This role focuses on organization-level billing and member management, excluding content-related access. ```typescript const billingManagerPermissions = [ "organization.read", "organization.manageBilling", "team.read", "organization.listMembers", ]; ``` -------------------------------- ### Server-Side Permission Check (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/pbac/README.md Demonstrates how to check a single or multiple permissions on the server-side using the PermissionCheckService. This is recommended for React Server Components or API routes. It includes fallback logic to the existing role system if PBAC is not enabled. ```typescript import { PermissionCheckService } from "@calcom/features/pbac/services/permission-check.service"; import { MembershipRole } from "@calcom/prisma/enums"; // In a Server Component or API route async function MyServerComponent({ teamId, userId }: { teamId: number; userId: number }) { const permissionService = new PermissionCheckService(); // Check a single permission const canUpdateTeam = await permissionService.checkPermission({ userId, teamId, permission: "team.update", fallbackRoles: [MembershipRole.OWNER, MembershipRole.ADMIN], }); // Check multiple permissions (user must have ALL) const canManageMembers = await permissionService.checkPermissions({ userId, teamId, permissions: ["team.invite", "team.remove"], fallbackRoles: [MembershipRole.OWNER, MembershipRole.ADMIN], }); if (!canUpdateTeam) { return
Not authorized
; } return
Team Settings
; } ``` -------------------------------- ### Reassign Booking Host (API) Source: https://context7.com/calcom/cal.com/llms.txt Reassigns a round-robin booking to a different host. This can be done automatically to an available host or specifically to a chosen user. The API call requires the booking UID and an authorization header. Reassigning to a specific user may include a reason for the reassignment. ```bash # Auto-reassign to available host curl -X POST https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings/bk_abc123xyz/reassign \ -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_***" ``` ```bash # Reassign to specific user curl -X POST https://api.cal.com/v2/bookings/bk_abc123xyz/reassign/5 \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "cal-api-version: 2024-08-13" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer cal_live_***" \ -d '{ "reassignReason": "Original host unavailable" }' ``` -------------------------------- ### Create tRPC Handler for Chart Data (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/developing/guides/insights/add-new-booking-charts.mdx This snippet illustrates how to create a tRPC endpoint to serve data for the new chart. It uses `userBelongsToTeamProcedure` for authentication and authorization, takes input from a schema, and calls a service method to retrieve data. Dependencies include `@calcom/trpc`, `@trpc/server`, and the relevant schema definitions. ```typescript // packages/features/insights/server/trpc-router.ts import { bookingRepositoryBaseInputSchema } from "@calcom/features/insights/server/raw-data.schema"; import { userBelongsToTeamProcedure } from "@calcom/trpc/server/procedures/authedProcedure"; import { TRPCError } from "@trpc/server"; export const insightsRouter = router({ // ... existing procedures myNewChartData: userBelongsToTeamProcedure .input(bookingRepositoryBaseInputSchema) .query(async ({ ctx, input }) => { // `createInsightsBookingService` is defined at the root level in this file const insightsBookingService = createInsightsBookingService(ctx, input); try { return await insightsBookingService.getMyNewChartData(); } catch (e) { throw new TRPCError({ code: "INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR" }); } }), }); ``` -------------------------------- ### Get All Resource Permissions (TypeScript) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/pbac/README.md Retrieves all available permissions for a specified resource using the PermissionCheckService. This method is efficient for checking multiple permissions at once but does not account for fallback roles. Ensure PBAC is enabled and rolled out before using. ```typescript import { PermissionCheckService } from "@calcom/features/pbac/services/permission-check.service"; import { Resource } from "@calcom/features/pbac/domain/types/permission-registry"; const permissionService = new PermissionCheckService(); // Get all permissions for a specific resource const permissions = await permissionService.getResourcePermissions({ userId, teamId, resource: Resource.EventType, }); // permissions = ["eventType.create", "eventType.read", "eventType.update", "eventType.delete"] ``` -------------------------------- ### Run Prisma Migrations (Bash) Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/pbac/README.md This command executes pending database migrations using Prisma. It's essential for applying schema changes, including the addition of new permissions defined in SQL migration files. ```bash npx prisma migrate dev ```
